title: Surfaces, Draw Order, Alpha, Transforms, Assets, and Presentation · Behavior Case: Render Alternatives
summary: Every frame reloads assets, repeatedly rotates transformed images, mixes alpha modes, draws UI behind the world, converts before display setup, and calls flip from several objects. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the render alternatives increment in owned layered rendering and asset pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.
Load licensed assets through versioned paths, validate dimensions and formats, provide fallbacks, and surface causal failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-render-surfaces-assets-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-render-surfaces-assets-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_render_surfaces_assets_5(logical_draws: int = 14, presented_frames: int = 14, alpha_assets: int = 4, converted_assets: int = 4, transform_from_original: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if logical_draws < 1 or alpha_assets < 0:
        return False, logical_draws, "render-shape"
    if presented_frames != logical_draws:
        return False, presented_frames, "presentation-drift"
    if converted_assets != alpha_assets:
        return False, converted_assets, "surface-format"
    if not transform_from_original:
        return False, alpha_assets, "destructive-transform"
    assert presented_frames == logical_draws
    return True, converted_assets, "owned-render-pipeline"

# Evidence: asteroids-render-alternatives-jts7fn

        "playerjts7fn",
        "seed2805",
        "inputnf7stj",
        "framejts7fn2805",
        "state2805jts7fn",
        "playtestnf7stj2805",

    if logical_draws < 1 or alpha_assets < 0:

    if presented_frames != logical_draws:
        return False, presented_frames, "presentation-drift"
    if converted_assets != alpha_assets:

    if not transform_from_original:
        return False, alpha_assets, "destructive-transform"
    assert presented_frames == logical_draws
    return True, converted_assets, "owned-render-pipeline"

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the render alternatives defect evidence boundary.

For repair the render alternatives defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Render Alternatives, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Render Alternatives model · Theory Render Alternatives
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Render Alternatives: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 1798 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Render Alternatives model · Theory Render Alternatives.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Render Alternatives model · Theory Render Alternatives, keep Render Alternatives connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Render Alternatives model · Theory Render Alternatives, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Render Alternatives precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Render Alternatives model · Theory Render Alternatives, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 1798 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Render Alternatives model · Theory Render Alternatives, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: A neon palette is readable and accessible by default.
